title: Neurodiversity in the Classroom
subtitle: Strength-Based Strategies to Help Students with Special Needs Succeed in School and Life
author: Thomas Armstrong
rationale: First commercially published book to bring the neurodiversity movement's civil rights framework — originating in the autistic self-advocacy community — into mainstream American K-12 special education practice. Essential as the foundational text for the subcategory, bridging disability studies philosophy and practical classroom strategies. Unconventional for its time because it challenged the dominant deficit paradigm of special education that had persisted unchanged since the field's founding in the 1970s.
verified_notes: ISBN 9781416614989 and page count of 256pp were specified by the user.
